capabilities.notes: Lexical path operations only; path values do not need to exist.
Reads host facts, environment variables, and PATH resolution without shelling out.
Exposes paths supplied by DslEvalContext; it does not inspect the filesystem.
Read-only existence/type predicates for absolute paths; no listing, reading, or mutation.

entries.name: build
entries.summary: Create a build action for tools compiled from source.

params.name: field
params.summary: Build fields such as `path`, `argv`, or ordered `argvs`.

entries.name: package

entries.returns: An action object with type `package`.

entries.name: required
entries.summary: Create an action for commands Scaffold should require but not install.
entries.returns: An action object with type `required`.

entries.name: archive
entries.summary: Create an action that extracts a local archive into the tool prefix.

params.name: path
params.summary: Archive path relative to the Scaffold root.

entries.returns: An action object with type `archive`. Supported formats are `.zip`, `.tar`, `.tar.gz`, `.tgz`, `.tar.bz2`, `.tbz2`, `.tar.xz`, `.txz`, and `.dmg`.

entries.name: archive/strip-components
entries.summary: Create an archive action that removes leading path components while extracting.

params.name: path
params.summary: Archive path relative to the Scaffold root.

params.name: count
params.summary: Number of leading path components to remove from archive entries.

entries.name: dmg
entries.summary: Create an action that mounts and extracts a local macOS DMG.

params.name: path
params.summary: DMG path relative to the Scaffold root.

entries.returns: An archive action for `.dmg` files. DMG extraction requires macOS `hdiutil` and `ditto`.

entries.name: bin
entries.summary: Describe a binary exposed by an installed tool.

entries.name: catalog

entries.returns: An object with a `tools` vector ready for Scaffold catalog output.

entries.name: catalog/tool
entries.summary: Create a raw catalog tool list for macro-oriented helpers.
entries.markdown: Prefer `tool` for ordinary catalog entries. Use `catalog/tool` when writing extension macros that need to splice fields directly into the raw catalog shape before Scaffold normalizes it.

params.name: field
params.summary: Additional raw fields added to the tool.

entries.returns: A raw association-list representation of a catalog tool.

entries.name: depends
entries.summary: Declare tools that must be installed before this tool.

params.name: name
params.summary: Tool name dependency. Requested installs include dependencies automatically.

entries.name: description
entries.summary: Create a longer metadata description for a tool.

entries.name: home-page
entries.summary: Create a metadata field for the tool's upstream homepage.

entries.name: host/package-platform
entries.summary: Create a package platform override for the current host OS.

params.name: requires
params.summary: Commands that must be available for this platform rule.

entries.name: host/package-platform-argvs
entries.summary: Create a package platform override with multiple commands for the current host OS.

params.name: requires
params.summary: Commands that must be available for this platform rule.

params.name: install-argvs
params.summary: Vector of install command argv vectors.

entries.name: host/uninstall-command
entries.summary: Create an uninstall command that only runs on matching hosts.

entries.name: host/uninstall-path
entries.summary: Create an uninstall path that is only removed on matching hosts.

entries.name: inherit
entries.summary: Derive a tool object by replacing fields on an existing tool.

entries.name: install/after
entries.summary: Declare tools that should be installed before this tool.

entries.name: install/before
entries.summary: Declare tools that should be installed after this tool.

entries.name: install/order
entries.summary: Set a numeric ordering priority for a tool within the resolved install graph.

params.name: value
params.summary: Lower values are installed earlier when dependency constraints allow.

entries.name: license
entries.summary: Create a metadata field for a tool license identifier or note.

entries.name: main-program
entries.summary: Create a metadata field naming the primary executable for a tool.

entries.name: maintainers
entries.summary: Create a metadata field listing maintainers for a catalog tool.

entries.name: meta
entries.summary: Attach descriptive metadata to a catalog tool.
entries.returns: A `meta` field accepted by `tool`.

entries.name: override
entries.summary: Derive a tool from overrides computed from the existing tool object.

params.name: proc
params.summary: Procedure that receives `base` and returns an object of replacement fields.

entries.name: passthru
entries.summary: Attach open extension data to a catalog tool.

params.name: field
params.summary: Arbitrary object fields preserved in evaluated catalog JSON but ignored by installation.

entries.name: required/tool
entries.summary: Create a raw catalog tool that must already exist on the host.
entries.markdown: This is the raw macro form behind higher-level required command helpers. Prefer `tool` with `required`, or an extension helper, unless a macro needs to splice raw fields.

params.name: field
params.summary: Additional raw fields added to the tool.

entries.returns: A raw tool list with a `required` action.

entries.name: source
entries.summary: Create a metadata field describing the upstream source location.

entries.name: tags
entries.summary: Create a metadata field listing searchable labels for a catalog tool.

entries.name: tool
entries.summary: Create a catalog tool object.

params.name: name
params.summary: Tool name used in catalog output.

params.name: action
params.summary: Action object describing how the tool is installed or required.

entries.returns: A tool object accepted by `catalog`.

entries.name: tool/append-bins
entries.summary: Append binary descriptors to an object's `bins` vector.

entries.name: tool/inherit
entries.summary: Facade name for deriving a tool object by replacing fields on an existing tool.

entries.name: tool/override
entries.summary: Facade name for deriving a tool from overrides computed from the existing tool object.

params.name: proc
params.summary: Procedure that receives `base` and returns an object of replacement fields.

entries.name: tool/platforms
entries.summary: Create a `platforms` field for a tool.

params.name: platform
params.summary: Host OS symbol or string such as `linux`, `macos`, or `windows`.

entries.name: tool/prefer-present
entries.summary: Create a required tool when the command is already present, otherwise use a package action.

params.name: name
params.summary: Command and tool name to prefer from the host when available.

params.summary: Package action used when the command is not available.

entries.returns: A tool object with either a `required` or package action.

entries.name: deprecated
entries.summary: Record replacement guidance and mark completions as deprecated.

entries.name: doc
entries.summary: Attach parseable documentation metadata to a value, macro, or DSL form.

params.name: subject
params.summary: Quoted symbol or string name of the documented entity.

params.name: field
params.summary: Structured metadata created by helpers such as `summary`, `param`, `returns`, and `example`.

entries.returns: A documentation object ignored as catalog output and indexed by editor tooling.

entries.name: doc-next
entries.summary: Attach documentation metadata to the following definition.

params.name: field
params.summary: Documentation metadata fields inherited by the following definition.

entries.returns: A documentation object indexed as if it documented the next binding.

entries.name: effect
entries.summary: Record the effect category for a module or documented binding.

entries.name: example
entries.summary: Attach a Scheme example that tooling renders as a code block.

entries.name: extern-doc
entries.summary: Attach documentation metadata to a host-backed or generated binding.

params.name: subject
params.summary: Symbol or string name of the documented external binding.

entries.returns: A documentation object normalized to a regular doc entry.

entries.name: group
entries.summary: Place a documented symbol into a tooling group.

entries.name: hidden
entries.summary: Hide an internal doc entry from public completion lists.

entries.name: markdown
entries.summary: Add longer Markdown documentation for hover and generated references.

entries.name: moduledoc
entries.summary: Attach parseable documentation metadata to a Scheme file or library.
entries.returns: A documentation object for file and library tooling.

entries.name: param
entries.summary: Document one parameter for signature help and generated references.

entries.name: requires-capability
entries.summary: Record a host capability required by a module or documented binding.

entries.name: returns
entries.summary: Document the result produced by a value or helper.

entries.name: see
entries.summary: Link a doc entry to a related symbol.

entries.name: signature
entries.summary: Declare the primary callable shape shown in completion and signature help.

entries.name: since
entries.summary: Record the Scaffold version that introduced the documented symbol.

entries.name: stability
entries.summary: Record stability guidance such as experimental or stable.

entries.name: summary
entries.summary: Provide the one-line summary used by completion lists and hover previews.

entries.name: typedoc
entries.summary: Attach parseable documentation metadata to a type-like DSL concept.

entries.name: directory/exists?
entries.summary: Return whether an absolute path exists and is a directory.
entries.returns: `#t` when the path exists as a directory, otherwise `#f`.

entries.name: file/exists?
entries.summary: Return whether an absolute path exists and is a regular file.
entries.returns: `#t` when the path exists as a file, otherwise `#f`.

entries.name: path/exists?
entries.summary: Return whether an absolute path exists on the host filesystem.
entries.returns: `#t` when the path exists, otherwise `#f`.

entries.name: command/available?
entries.summary: Return whether a command is available on the current host.
entries.returns: `#t` when the command is detected, otherwise `#f`.

entries.name: command/path
entries.summary: Resolve a command name to an executable path on the current host.
entries.returns: The resolved executable path as a string, or `#f` when absent.

entries.name: command/path?
entries.summary: Return whether a command resolves to an executable path.
entries.returns: `#t` when the command resolves, otherwise `#f`.

entries.name: env/var
entries.summary: Return an environment variable from the current Scaffold process.
entries.returns: The variable value as a string, or `#f` when it is absent.

entries.name: env/var?
entries.summary: Return whether an environment variable is set for the current Scaffold process.
entries.returns: `#t` when the variable is set, otherwise `#f`.

entries.name: host/arch
entries.summary: Symbol for the current CPU architecture.

entries.name: host/matches?
entries.summary: Return whether a platform target matches the current host.

params.name: target
params.summary: An OS symbol, a platform string, or a predicate object.

entries.returns: `#t` when the target matches the detected host, otherwise `#f`.

entries.name: host/os
entries.summary: Symbol for the current operating system.
entries.returns: A host OS symbol such as `linux`, populated by Scaffold before evaluation.

entries.name: host/platform
entries.summary: String describing the detected host platform.

entries.name: begin
entries.summary: Evaluate expressions in order and return the final value.
entries.markdown: Evaluate expressions in order and return the final value.

entries.name: cond
entries.summary: Branch across ordered test clauses.

entries.name: define
entries.summary: Bind a Scheme value or procedure in the current scope.
entries.markdown: Bind a Scheme value or procedure in the current scope.

entries.name: define-syntax
entries.summary: Bind a hygienic syntax transformer in the current scope.
entries.markdown: Bind a hygienic syntax transformer in the current scope.

entries.name: export
entries.summary: Export bindings from a Scheme library.
entries.markdown: Export bindings from a Scheme library.

entries.name: if
entries.summary: Choose between consequent and alternate expressions.
entries.markdown: Choose between consequent and alternate expressions.

entries.name: import
entries.summary: Import Scheme libraries allowed by Scaffold's import policy.
entries.markdown: Import Scheme libraries allowed by Scaffold's import policy.

entries.name: lambda
entries.summary: Create a Scheme procedure.

entries.name: let
entries.summary: Bind local values for a body expression.
entries.markdown: Bind local values for a body expression.

entries.name: let*
entries.summary: Bind local values sequentially for a body expression.
entries.markdown: Bind local values sequentially for a body expression.

entries.name: letrec
entries.summary: Bind mutually recursive local values for a body expression.
entries.markdown: Bind mutually recursive local values for a body expression.

entries.name: library
entries.summary: Define a Scheme library for a local Scaffold extension.
entries.markdown: Define a Scheme library for a local Scaffold extension.

entries.name: quote
entries.summary: Return datum without evaluating it.

entries.name: syntax-rules
entries.summary: Define a hygienic Scheme macro.

entries.name: nix/argv
entries.summary: Append Nix subcommands and flags to the shared Nix argv prefix.
entries.returns: Vector argv beginning with `nix --extra-experimental-features`.

entries.name: nix/base-argv
entries.summary: Base argv prefix for all Nix helpers.
entries.returns: Vector argv for `nix --extra-experimental-features ...`.

entries.name: nix/experimental-features
entries.summary: Default Nix experimental feature string used by this module.
entries.returns: `"nix-command flakes"`.

params.summary: Installable refs; defaults to `{{ package }}` when omitted.

entries.name: field
entries.summary: Create one object field pair.

params.name: name
params.summary: Symbol or string key to store in the object.

params.name: value
params.summary: Any Scheme value that should become the field value.

entries.returns: A pair consumed by `object`, merge helpers, and catalog constructors.

entries.name: field/name
entries.summary: Return the name part of a field pair.

entries.name: field/value
entries.summary: Return the value part of a field pair.

entries.name: object
entries.summary: Create a Scaffold object from field pairs.
entries.returns: A Scheme list of pairs that serializes as a JSON object.

entries.name: object/append-field-vector
entries.summary: Append vector values to an object field.

entries.name: object/append-vector
entries.summary: Append values to an object vector field.

entries.name: object/has-field?
entries.summary: Return whether an object contains a field.

entries.name: object/inherit
entries.summary: Create a derived object by replacing fields on a base object.

entries.name: object/merge
entries.summary: Merge objects with later fields replacing earlier fields.

entries.name: object/override
entries.summary: Create a derived object from overrides computed from the base object.

params.name: proc
params.summary: Procedure that receives `base` and returns an object of replacement fields.

entries.name: object/ref
entries.summary: Read a field value from an object with an optional default.

params.name: obj
params.summary: Object created with `object` or a catalog helper.

params.name: default
params.summary: Optional value returned when the field is absent.

entries.returns: The field value, the optional default, or `#f` when no default is supplied.

entries.name: object/remove-fields
entries.summary: Return an object with named fields removed.

entries.name: object/replace-field
entries.summary: Replace one named field on an object.

entries.name: object/replace-fields
entries.summary: Return an object with fields replaced by name.

entries.name: path/absolute?
entries.summary: Return whether a path is absolute on the current host.

entries.name: path/extension
entries.summary: Return the final extension component of a path.
entries.returns: The extension as a string without a leading dot, or `#f`.

entries.name: path/file-name
entries.summary: Return the final file name component of a path.
entries.returns: The file name as a string, or `#f` when no file name exists.

entries.name: path/join
entries.summary: Join path components using the current host path rules.
entries.returns: A string path. Absolute later components may replace earlier components.

entries.name: path/normalize
entries.summary: Lexically normalize a path without reading the filesystem.
entries.returns: A normalized path string.

entries.name: path/parent
entries.summary: Return the parent directory of a path.
entries.returns: The parent path as a string, or `#f` when no parent exists.

entries.name: path/relative?
entries.summary: Return whether a path is relative on the current host.

entries.name: path/separator
entries.summary: Directory separator string for paths on the current host.

entries.name: package/platform
entries.summary: Create a platform-specific package install rule with required commands.

entries.name: package/platform-argvs
entries.summary: Create a platform-specific package install rule with multiple commands.

params.name: predicate
params.summary: Host predicate for this package rule.

params.name: requires
params.summary: Commands that must be available for this platform rule.

params.name: install-argvs
params.summary: Vector of install command argv vectors.

entries.name: platform/package
entries.summary: Create a platform-specific package override.

entries.name: predicate
entries.summary: Create an OS/architecture predicate for platform-specific catalog data.

entries.name: tool/path
entries.summary: Describe a platform-specific path where a tool may already exist.

entries.name: distrobox/enter-argv
entries.summary: Wrap argv so it runs through `distrobox enter <box> --`.

params.name: argv
params.summary: Command vector to run inside the container.

entries.returns: Wrapped argv vector.

entries.name: distrobox/host-exec-argv
entries.summary: Wrap argv so a Distrobox process runs it on the host.

entries.name: distrobox/package
entries.summary: Rewrite a package action so install commands run inside a Distrobox.
entries.returns: A package action with install argv fields wrapped for the container.

entries.name: distrobox/tool
entries.summary: Rewrite a tool so install, check, and uninstall commands run inside a Distrobox.
entries.returns: A tool object with install, check, and uninstall argv wrapped.

entries.name: assert/equal
entries.summary: Assert that two Scheme values are equal.
entries.markdown: Raises an assertion violation containing both values when they differ.

params.name: actual
params.summary: Actual value produced by the code under test.

entries.returns: `#t` when the values compare with `equal?`.

entries.name: assert/true
entries.summary: Assert that a value is truthy.
entries.markdown: Raises an assertion violation named `assert/true` when the value is false.

params.name: value
params.summary: Expression result that must not be `#f`.

entries.returns: `#t` when the assertion passes.

entries.name: argv/append
entries.summary: Append a Scheme list of argv items to an argv vector.

entries.name: install-argv/prepend
entries.summary: Prepend argv parts to an object's `install-argv` vector.

entries.name: package/map-install-argvs
entries.summary: Apply a transformation to all install argv fields in a package action.

params.name: package-action
params.summary: Package action that may contain `install-argv`, `install-argvs`, or platform overrides.

params.name: proc
params.summary: Procedure that receives and returns an argv vector.

entries.name: tool/map-check-argvs
entries.summary: Transform each check argv vector in a tool.

entries.name: tool/map-package-install-argvs
entries.summary: Transform package install argv fields inside a tool's action.

entries.name: tool/map-uninstall-command-argvs
entries.summary: Transform uninstall command argv fields inside a tool.

entries.name: uninstall/map-command-argvs
entries.summary: Transform each uninstall command argv vector.

params.name: proc
params.summary: Procedure that receives and returns an argv vector.

entries.name: arr
entries.summary: Create a Scaffold vector value.
entries.returns: A Scheme vector that serializes as a JSON array.

entries.name: arr/append-list
entries.summary: Append a Scheme list to a Scaffold vector.

entries.name: arr/prepend-list
entries.summary: Prepend a Scheme list to a Scaffold vector.

entries.name: vector/append
entries.summary: Append any number of vectors into one vector.

entries.name: vector/map
entries.summary: Map a procedure over a vector and return a vector.

entries.name: cmd/c-argv
entries.summary: Build argv for running a command through `cmd /C`.
entries.returns: Vector argv beginning with `cmd`.

entries.name: cmd/tool
entries.summary: Create a required descriptor for `cmd`.

entries.name: powershell/command-argv
entries.summary: Build argv for running a legacy Windows PowerShell command without profile loading.
entries.returns: Vector argv beginning with `powershell`.

entries.name: powershell/tool
entries.summary: Create a required descriptor for legacy Windows PowerShell `powershell`.

entries.name: pwsh/command-argv
entries.summary: Build argv for running a modern PowerShell command without profile loading.
entries.returns: Vector argv beginning with `pwsh`.

entries.name: pwsh/tool
entries.summary: Create a required descriptor for modern cross-platform PowerShell `pwsh`.

entries.name: reg/add-argv
entries.summary: Build argv for adding or replacing one Windows Registry value.

params.name: type
params.summary: Registry data type such as `REG_SZ` or `REG_DWORD`.

entries.returns: Vector argv for `reg add /f`.

entries.name: reg/query-argv
entries.summary: Build argv for querying one Windows Registry value.
entries.returns: Vector argv for `reg query`.

entries.name: reg/tool
entries.summary: Create a required descriptor for Windows Registry CLI `reg`.

entries.name: where/argv
entries.summary: Build argv for locating a command on Windows PATH.
entries.returns: Vector argv for `where`.

entries.name: where/tool
entries.summary: Create a required descriptor for command lookup tool `where`.

entries.name: windows/command-tool
entries.summary: Create a Windows-only required command descriptor.
entries.returns: A required catalog tool constrained to Windows.

entries.name: source/dir
entries.summary: Directory of the Scheme source currently being evaluated.
entries.returns: A path string, or `#f` when no source path is available.

entries.name: source/path
entries.summary: Path of the Scheme source currently being evaluated.
entries.returns: A path string, or `#f` when no source path is available.

entries.name: workspace/path
entries.summary: Join path components under `workspace/root`.

params.name: part
params.summary: Path component appended under the workspace root.

entries.returns: A path string under `workspace/root`.

entries.name: workspace/root
entries.summary: Root directory used for the current Scaffold evaluation.
entries.returns: A path string, or `#f` when no workspace root is available.

entries.name: macos/command-tool
entries.summary: Create a macOS-only required command descriptor.
entries.returns: A required catalog tool constrained to macOS.

entries.name: npm/global-install-argv
entries.summary: Build argv for globally installing an npm package.

params.name: flag
params.summary: Additional npm install flags placed before the package name.

entries.returns: Vector argv for `npm install --global`.

entries.name: npm/global-package-platform
entries.summary: Create a package/platform override that installs with global npm.

params.name: predicate
params.summary: Host predicate for this package rule.

params.name: option
params.summary: Additional npm install flags or package platform fields. Field values are applied after defaults.

entries.name: npm/global-tool
entries.summary: Create a catalog tool installed globally with npm.

params.name: option
params.summary: Additional npm install flags or tool fields. Field values are applied after defaults.

entries.name: npm/global-uninstall-argv
entries.summary: Build argv for uninstalling a global npm package.
entries.returns: Vector argv for `npm uninstall --global`.

entries.name: npx/argv
entries.summary: Build argv for running a package through `npx`.

params.name: argv
params.summary: Arguments forwarded to the package command.

entries.name: uv/tool
entries.summary: Create a catalog tool installed with `uv tool install`.

params.name: name
params.summary: Python package name and default catalog tool name.

params.name: option
params.summary: Additional `uv tool install` flags or tool fields. Field values are applied after defaults.

entries.name: uv/tool-install-argv
entries.summary: Build argv for installing or upgrading a Python tool with uv.

params.name: package-name
params.summary: Package name passed to `uv tool install`.

params.name: flag
params.summary: Additional `uv tool install` flags placed before the package name.

entries.returns: Vector argv using `uv tool install --upgrade --force`.

entries.name: uv/tool-platform
entries.summary: Create a package/platform override that installs with `uv tool install`.

params.name: predicate
params.summary: Host predicate for this package rule.

params.name: option
params.summary: Additional `uv tool install` flags or package platform fields. Field values are applied after defaults.

entries.name: uv/tool-uninstall-argv
entries.summary: Build argv for uninstalling a Python tool with uv.

params.name: tool-name
params.summary: Tool name passed to `uv tool uninstall`.

entries.returns: Vector argv for `uv tool uninstall`.

entries.name: uvx/argv
entries.summary: Build argv for running a Python package through `uvx`.

params.name: argv
params.summary: Arguments forwarded to the package command.
